item.title: the rendering reference
item.description: every block this site knows how to render, in one scroll — listings, the checksec panel, admonitions, spoilers, tables, footnotes. The other seed post is the gentle tour; this one is the exhaustive list, and my regression test.

item.title: rizin and radare2 in anger
item.description: The terminal reverse-engineering workflow — open, analyse, navigate, search, patch, and debug a binary without ever leaving the prompt. The commands I actually use, plus where rizin and radare2 diverge.

item.title: a pwntools cookbook
item.description: The pwntools I reach for from memory — the template, tubes, packing, the ELF/libc helpers, format-string and shellcode one-liners, and the CLI subcommands that replace a pile of little scripts. Real snippets, copy-paste shaped.

item.title: one keybind to checksec everything
item.description: A small workflow I paste into every CTF box — pull every binary's mitigations into one scannable list, run it from a single keybind, and stop re-typing checksec for the hundredth time. Glue, not genius.

item.title: finding gadgets: ROPgadget, ropper, one_gadget, pwntools
item.description: NX means you reuse the code that's already there. Here are the tools that find the pieces, the pwntools layer that assembles them into a chain, and the three structured techniques for when a clean `pop rdi; ret` just isn't in the binary.

item.title: pwndbg muscle memory
item.description: The gdb + pwndbg commands I actually type, grouped the way I reach for them rather than the way the docs list them. Heap inspection, the search primitives, breaking under PIE, and the three-command pre-flight before every session.

item.title: the first five minutes with an unknown binary
item.description: A fixed triage I run on every new binary before I open a disassembler — identify, enumerate, watch it run. The exact commands, in order, and what each one is actually telling you.

item.title: anti-debug bingo: five checks and how they fall
item.description: A survey of the anti-debug checks you trip over on Linux RE targets — what each one measures, the one line that gives it away, and the cheapest defeat. Folded one per card so you can skim to the one that's biting you right now.

item.title: two signatures, one nonce: recovering an ECDSA key
item.description: The signing oracle reused a nonce across two messages. That's the whole vulnerability — and it collapses the private key to a couple of modular divisions. The algebra, then the generalisation to merely *biased* nonces and a lattice.

item.title: a slab use-after-free, from ioctl to root
item.description: A toy char driver frees an object and keeps the pointer. From there it's the usual kernel kata — reclaim the slot with a spray, leak KASLR through the dangling read, then turn the dangling write into a modprobe_path overwrite.

item.title: devirtualizing a toy VM obfuscator
item.description: VM-based obfuscation looks terrifying until you realize it's just an interpreter you haven't reverse engineered yet. A walk through recovering the bytecode of a custom virtual machine. Educational target.

item.title: ret2libc on a warm-up: stack smash to shell
item.description: A 200-point warm-up — one unbounded read into a 64-byte stack buffer. No canary, but PIE and ASLR are on, so the leak comes first. Classic ret2libc, written down end to end.

item.title: tcache poisoning, explained like you'll have to use it
item.description: a from-scratch walk through the most reliable beginner heap primitive — what the tcache is, why poisoning works, and how safe-linking changed the recipe. Educational target, not a real CVE.

item.title: this site is a folder of text files
item.description: how this blog is built, how to add a post, and a tour of every feature you get for free. Delete this post once you've read it.

item.title: advent of pwn 2025
item.description: A December advent calendar of pwn and reversing challenges — one door a day, full solves folded behind spoilers. Day 1 byte-diffs Santa's naughty-or-nice list; day 2 coaxes a holiday daemon into dumping coal.
